name: Gnocchi Di Susine
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the whole potatoes until they are soft (about 45 minutes). While still warm, peel and pass through a vegetable mill onto clean pasta board.
2. Make a well in the center of the mashed potatoes and sprinkle all over with flour, using all the flour. Place egg and salt in the center of the well and stir gently into the flour and potatoes until combined, like making normal pasta dough.
3. Once the egg is mixed in, bring the dough together, kneading gently until a ball forms. Knead another 4 minutes until the ball is dry to the touch.
4. To make Gnocchi di Susine:
- Bring 6 quarts of water to boil and add 2 tablespoons of salt.
- Pinch off walnut-sized balls of gnocchi dough and roll with a rolling pin to form 2-1/2 inch circles of dough.
- Place one quarter of plum and a 1/2 teaspoon sugar on each gnocchi piece, then fold the dough around the plum pieces to form a package without tears or rips, evenly coated.
- Continue until all are finished. If making pasta ahead, cook immediately and chill in cold water.
5. To serve: Place gnocchi in boiling water in batches of 10, stirring gently to prevent sticking to the sides or bottom of the pan. Allow to float for 2 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, melt butter in a 12-inch saute pan over medium heat. Add bread crumbs and then add cooked gnocchi, tossing gently to coat. Serve on a plate and sprinkle with cinnamon.
Yield: 4 servings
